_meta.source: Scripture references paired with Catechism of the Catholic Church (CCC) paragraph numbers
_meta.usage: Engine reads this file to display scripture at narrative moments, loading screens, item tooltips, battle events, death/resurrection, moral choices, and virtue milestones

verses.text: Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me.
Blessed are those who are persecuted for righteousness' sake, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
If anyone would come after me, let him deny himself and take up his cross and follow me.
Greater love has no one than this, that someone lay down his life for his friends.
The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it.
We know that in everything God works for good with those who love him, who are called according to his purpose.

Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall tribulation, or distress, or persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword? No, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him who loved us.
Now faith is the assurance of things hoped for, the conviction of things not seen.

Therefore, since we are surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses, let us also lay aside every weight, and sin which clings so closely, and let us run with perseverance the race that is set before us.
verses._note: THIS is the theological foundation of the cross-era mechanic. The Cloud of Witnesses IS the gameplay loop.

verses.text: Be faithful unto death, and I will give you the crown of life.
Truly I say to you, as you did it to one of the least of these my brethren, you did it to me.
So faith by itself, if it has no works, is dead.

triggers._note: Catholic emphasis: faith AND works, not sola fide

verses.text: But the souls of the righteous are in the hand of God, and no torment will ever touch them. In the eyes of the foolish they seemed to have died, and their departure was thought to be an affliction, and their going from us to be their destruction; but they are at peace.
verses._note: Deuterocanonical — Wisdom of Solomon. Explicitly Catholic canon.

verses.text: My son, if you come forward to serve the Lord, prepare yourself for temptation. Set your heart right and be steadfast, and do not be hasty in time of calamity. Cleave to him and do not depart, that you may be honored at the end of your life. Accept whatever is brought upon you, and in changes that humble you be patient. For gold is tested in the fire, and acceptable men in the furnace of humiliation.
verses._note: Deuterocanonical — Sirach/Ecclesiasticus. Key Catholic text on endurance.

verses.text: Give some of your food to the hungry, and some of your clothing to the naked. Give all your surplus to charity, and do not let your eye begrudge the gift.
verses._note: Deuterocanonical — Tobit. Corporal works of mercy.

verses.text: I beg you, my child, to look at the heaven and the earth and see everything that is in them, and recognize that God did not make them out of things that existed. So also mankind comes into being. Do not fear this butcher, but prove worthy of your brothers. Accept death, so that in God's mercy I may get you back again with your brothers.

triggers._note: The Maccabean mother — direct parallel to Obaa-chan. Deuterocanonical.

verses.text: Our Father, who art in heaven, hallowed be thy name. Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done, on earth as it is in heaven. Give us this day our daily bread, and forgive us our trespasses, as we forgive those who trespass against us. And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil.
Hail, full of grace, the Lord is with thee. Blessed art thou among women, and blessed is the fruit of thy womb.
He who eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day. For my flesh is food indeed, and my blood is drink indeed.

triggers._note: Real Presence — central Catholic doctrine. The hidden masses ARE the game.

verses.text: For where two or three are gathered in my name, there am I in the midst of them.
But I say to you, love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you.
I can do all things through him who strengthens me.
The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom, and the knowledge of the Holy One is insight.
He has showed you, O man, what is good; and what does the Lord require of you but to do justice, and to love kindness, and to walk humbly with your God?
Watch and pray that you may not enter into temptation; the spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.
No temptation has overtaken you that is not common to man. God is faithful, and he will not let you be tempted beyond your strength, but with the temptation will also provide the way of escape.
Fear not, for I am with you; be not dismayed, for I am your God; I will strengthen you, I will help you, I will uphold you with my victorious right hand.
And behold, I am with you always, to the close of the age.
Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you; not as the world gives do I give to you. Let not your hearts be troubled, neither let them be afraid.
Father, forgive them; for they know not what they do.
Be sober, be watchful. Your adversary the devil prowls around like a roaring lion, seeking someone to devour.

Put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to stand against the wiles of the devil. For we are not contending against flesh and blood, but against the principalities, against the powers, against the world rulers of this present darkness.
The steadfast love of the Lord never ceases, his mercies never come to an end; they are new every morning.
Into your hands I commit my spirit.

triggers._note: Christ's last words on the cross. Also Stephen's. Also every martyr's.

verses.text: Where shall I go from your Spirit? Or where shall I flee from your presence? If I ascend to heaven, you are there! If I make my bed in Sheol, you are there! If I take the wings of the morning and dwell in the uttermost parts of the sea, even there your hand shall lead me.
There is no fear in love, but perfect love casts out fear.
Ask, and it will be given you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.
And let us not grow weary in well-doing, for in due season we shall reap, if we do not lose heart.
I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.
My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.
I am the light of the world; he who follows me will not walk in darkness, but will have the light of life.
And as they were stoning Stephen, he prayed, 'Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.' And he knelt down and cried with a loud voice, 'Lord, do not hold this sin against them.'

triggers._note: First Christian martyr. Pattern for every martyr that follows.

verses.text: Watch and pray that you may not enter into temptation; the spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.
I am the resurrection and the life; he who believes in me, though he die, yet shall he live, and whoever lives and believes in me shall never die.

prayers._note: Named Catholic prayers used in-game as liturgical anchors

pater_noster.name: Our Father
pater_noster.context: Recited at every hidden Mass. The prayer that unites all eras.
pater_noster.game_use: Restores party HP during rest at sacred sites. Stronger with faith virtue.

ave_maria.name: Hail Mary
ave_maria.context: Whispered by Kakure Kirishitan using Japanese words. Orasho tradition.
ave_maria.game_use: Marian protection — reduces damage from next attack.

salve_regina.name: Hail Holy Queen
salve_regina.context: Sung in catacombs. A prayer of exiles and pilgrims.
salve_regina.game_use: Restores hope in desolation scenes. Prevents despair status.

act_of_contrition.name: Act of Contrition
act_of_contrition.context: Before confession. Used when Usagi has made a harmful choice.
act_of_contrition.game_use: Can partially reverse negative virtue effects from poor moral choices.

anima_christi.name: Soul of Christ
anima_christi.context: Post-communion prayer. Ignatius of Loyola's favorite.

memorare.name: The Memorare
memorare.context: Prayer of desperation to the Blessed Virgin. 'Never was it known...'
memorare.game_use: Emergency miracle — usable once per book when all seems lost.

de_profundis.name: Out of the Depths
de_profundis.context: Prayer from the abyss. Psalm of ascents.
de_profundis.game_use: Revives from death screen if faith is high enough.

loading_screen.style: centered, italic serif, gold on dark, fade in/out 2s each
loading_screen.selection: random from eligible verses matching current book/act

moment_overlay.style: bottom screen overlay, white text, smaller reference right-aligned

battle_flash.style: brief center flash, larger text, gold, no reference shown

virtue_milestone.style: top of screen toast, gold border, verse + virtue icon

death_screen.style: full screen dark overlay, centered verse, gentle pulse, reference below

item_tooltip.style: italicized quote below item description, smaller text, muted gold
